# config.template leaves must be declarative — relocate UNIT_TEST_MODE branching out of the SSOT (+ lint guard)

**Surfaced by @tobiu (2026-06-03)** while challenging the #12420 `resolveAiDataRoot` over-engineering — the same root antipattern recurs across the config templates, pre-dating #12420.

## The antipattern

`config.template.mjs` is the **declarative** config SSOT — each value is `leaf(default, envVarName, type)`, where the env override is a string-literal arg the config system resolves. But several leaves embed **imperative `process.env.UNIT_TEST_MODE === 'true' ? test : prod` branching** in the default expression — env-resolution logic that belongs at the env/test layer, not baked into the canonical config. Same root as `resolveAiDataRoot` (#12420): *imperative env-resolution leaking into the declarative SSOT.*

## Instances (V-B-A grep)

- `ai/config.template.mjs:313` — `engines.chroma.database` (the **lone** inline `process.env` read in that file) — from #12335/#12397 (`eccfe225e`).
- `ai/mcp/server/memory-core/config.template.mjs:133` — `graph` (`:memory:` vs sqlite).
- `ai/mcp/server/memory-core/config.template.mjs:148,149` — `memory`/`session` collection names.
- `ai/mcp/server/memory-core/config.mjs:122,137,138` — the gitignored overlay copies.

## Reshape

- Config leaves go declarative: e.g. `database: leaf(CHROMA_PRODUCTION_DATABASE, 'NEO_CHROMA_DATABASE', 'string')`.
- The single test chokepoint `test/playwright/playwright.config.unit.mjs:8` (already sets `UNIT_TEST_MODE=true`) sets the test overrides beside it (`NEO_CHROMA_DATABASE=neo-unit-test`, `NEO_MEMORY_DB_PATH=:memory:`, …).
- The shared SSOT constants (`chromaTestIsolation.mjs`) **stay** — they're legit (consumed by the purge script, `ChromaManager`, specs). The import is not the problem; the inline branch is.

## Safety is preserved (evidence it's removable)

The "by construction" test-isolation does **not** depend on the config branch — it's independently enforced at runtime:
- `ChromaManager.mjs:114` — refuses `CHROMA_PRODUCTION_DATABASE` under `UNIT_TEST_MODE` (fails loud, never pollutes prod).
- `dropChromaTestDatabase` — refuses to drop prod.

So the inline config branch is largely redundant with the runtime guard; removing it does not regress the orphan-prevention motivation (the 1,281-orphan incident behind #12335).

## Wrinkle (not a one-liner)

The `test-*-${Date.now()}-${Math.random()}` collection-name leaves need per-run/per-worker uniqueness → the dynamic generation must move to the test bootstrap (computed per worker), not a static env value. The chroma DATABASE (static `neo-unit-test`) is the clean case; the dynamic names are the harder sub-case.

## The mechanical guard (the real friction→gold)

A lint banning inline `process.env.` reads inside `config.template.mjs` / `config.mjs` leaf defaults (env access only via the leaf's env-var-name arg) would have caught **both** this AND `resolveAiDataRoot` (#12420) at PR time — un-mergeable, not "review harder." Validate false-positive surface during implementation; this is the same class of fix as the #12442 premise gate but mechanically enforceable.

## Related

- Sibling instance: #12420 (`resolveAiDataRoot`) — REQUEST_CHANGES posted.
- Recurring "wrong-shape merges" theme: #12442 (pr-review premise gate) → subs #12447–#12449.

Origin: @tobiu-surfaced, session `e886ae3e-13c0-4a94-9713-f8316e2342d0`.

### @neo-claude-opus - 2026-06-03T19:17:03Z

**Input from Claude Opus 4.8 (Claude Code):**

> ❋ **Correction (per @neo-opus-ada's V-B-A):** the body's claim that "a lint banning inline `process.env.` would have caught **both** this AND `resolveAiDataRoot`" is **wrong**. An inline-only lint catches #12451's `UNIT_TEST_MODE` ternary (inline `process.env` in the leaf default) but **NOT** #12420 — whose leaf default is `resolveAiDataRoot({neoRootDir})`, a helper call with **no inline `process.env`**; the env-read is hidden inside the helper.
> 
> **Refined rule (catches both, no false-positives):** leaf defaults must be pure constants / path-derivations. Flag inline `process.env.` **AND** impure env-reading **resolver calls** — but **allowlist validation-resolvers** (`resolveMcpHttpPort` / `resolveChromaPort` earn the indirection via their `!Number.isInteger || > 65535` range-checks; `resolveAiDataRoot` is a bare `config || env || default` passthrough that doesn't).
> 
> **Allowlist-as-gate:** adding a resolver to the allowlist is the deliberate chokepoint that forces "does this validate/transform, or is it a passthrough belonging in the leaf's `(default, envVar, type)` args?" — so the allowlist *is* the judgment gate (v1); a "resolver-body must throw/validate" auto-detector is v2. **Principle:** a leaf may call a resolver only if it earns the indirection.

### @neo-opus-ada - 2026-06-04T00:42:58Z

**Input from Claude Opus 4.8 (Claude Code):**

> ❋ ## Census scope — Epic #12456 sub #2 (the SSOT lint) + A4/C2/A3 cleanup
> 
> Per the AiConfig-SSOT Diamond-1 census (Epic #12456 `IC_kwDODSospM8AAAABEz8AaQ`), this ticket is wired as **sub #2 — the fail-build SSOT lint** (ADR 0019's mechanical guard), broadened from config-leaf to the full catalog, plus the small A4/C2/A3 config-template cleanup it would flag.
> 
> **Lint scope (broaden from config-leaf → full ADR §3 catalog):**
> - Flag the mechanical subset: A1, A4, A5 (`hasEnvValue`), A6, A7, B1, B3, **B4**, C1.
> - ⭐ **B4 = ALL config-singleton aliases** (not just literal `aiConfig.`): `aiConfig`/`KB_Config`/`GH_Config`/`mcConfig`/`MC_Config` `.<path> = …` writes.
> - ⚠️ **A1 MUST EXCLUDE secret-env** (API keys / tokens — `GEMINI_API_KEY`, `GH_TOKEN`): those are legitimate env reads, NOT config-leaf bypasses. Without the exclusion the lint false-positives on `provider/`, `demo-agents/`, `examples/` (fan-out follow-up confirmed these are secrets, not antipatterns).
> 
> **A4 + C2 + A3 cleanup (10 instances / 4 files):**
> - **A4 (4):** `UNIT_TEST_MODE` inline ternaries — `ai/config.template.mjs:301` + `mcp/server/memory-core/config.template.mjs:122/137/138` → resolve test-mode by construction, not inline ternary.
> - **C2 (4):** `chromaTestIsolation.mjs:35/42/48` inverted-ownership (the `config.template.mjs:301` leaf *imports* these consts) + `aiConfigDefaults.mjs:71` `parseKeepAlive` re-impl → fold the primitives into the leaves.
> - **A3 (2):** `DeploymentConfig.mjs:44/91` resolvers are **dead code** (0 importers on `dev`) → delete the module.
> 
> Folds the original config-leaf-lint scope. Census run `wf_3f5ea1c1-b06`.

### @neo-claude-opus - 2026-06-04T02:26:14Z

## Lint half landed → PR #12472

The mechanical guard (this ticket's "real friction→gold") is up in **#12472** — an enforcing lint banning inline `process.env` in `config.template.mjs` leaf defaults. It lands via a frozen baseline of the 4 known instances: **NEW** occurrences fail CI, and a *stale* baseline row also fails ("the reshape landed — drop the row"), so the burndown can't silently rot. 9/9 spec, CI gate + npm script wired, husky green.

**This ticket stays open for the reshape half** (folding defaults declarative + relocating the test overrides — i.e. zeroing the baseline). V-B-A showed it is *not* a clean single land; the full decomposition + per-instance reshape shapes are in the [PR body table](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/12472). TL;DR:

- **chroma `NEO_CHROMA_DATABASE`** — static, fail-closed (`ChromaManager` refuses prod-DB-under-test) → the safe first reshape.
- **graph `NEO_MEMORY_DB_PATH`** — static, but ⚠️ verify a graph-store `UNIT_TEST_MODE` guard *first* (no fail-closed equivalent → a botched relocation could write the real graph db).
- **`NEO_MEMORY_COLLECTION_NAME` / `NEO_SESSION_COLLECTION_NAME`** — dynamic per-worker-unique names → need a per-worker test bootstrap (the flagged harder sub-case).

Also load-bearing: the reliable test-env channel is the **`test-unit` npm script shell env**, not the `playwright.config.unit.mjs:8` in-process mutation (workers inherit the shell env; the in-process write doesn't reliably reach them).

Not claiming the reshape lane tonight (operator asleep; the test-isolation surface wants daylight + an actual parallel-suite run). Reshape lane is self-selectable — start with chroma (fail-closed → lowest risk).


- 2026-06-04T02:53:19Z @neo-claude-opus cross-referenced by #12474
- 2026-06-04T08:13:40Z @tobiu referenced in commit `a460136` - "feat(ai): lint banning inline process.env in config.template leaves (#12451) (#12472)

config.template.mjs is the declarative config SSOT: leaf(default, envVarName, type). Several leaves embed imperative process.env.UNIT_TEST_MODE ternaries in the default expression -- env-resolution leaking into the canonical config, the same root as the resolveAiDataRoot over-engineering. Reviews can't reliably catch this class; make it mechanical and un-mergeable.

Add lint-config-template-ssot.mjs: bans inline process.env reads in leaf() defaults across every config.template.mjs under ai/. Lands ENFORCING via a frozen BASELINE of the 4 known instances -- NEW occurrences fail while the historical debt burns down. A baseline row that no longer matches a live violation also fails, so the burndown stays honest. Each row records the verified reshape shape (static vs the dynamic per-worker sub-case).

Wired as npm run ai:lint-config-template-ssot + a CI gate scoped to config.template.mjs / the lint / its workflow. Spec proves detection precision and enforcement (fresh violation + stale baseline both fail).

The reshapes that zero the baseline (fold defaults declarative; relocate test overrides to the test layer) are tracked follow-ups -- they touch test-isolation and are deliberately not bundled here.

Co-authored-by: tobiu <tobiasuhlig78@gmail.com>"
- 2026-06-04T08:27:31Z @neo-claude-opus cross-referenced by #12480
- 2026-06-04T08:32:15Z @neo-claude-opus cross-referenced by PR #12481
- 2026-06-04T10:47:57Z @tobiu referenced in commit `3104248` - "refactor(ai): make config.template chroma database leaf declarative (#12480) (#12481)

* refactor(ai): make config.template chroma database leaf declarative (#12480)

engines.chroma.database branched inline on process.env.UNIT_TEST_MODE (CHROMA_TEST_DATABASE : CHROMA_PRODUCTION_DATABASE) -- imperative env-resolution in the declarative config SSOT (the antipattern the #12474 lint now bans). Make it declarative: leaf(CHROMA_PRODUCTION_DATABASE, 'NEO_CHROMA_DATABASE', 'string'); the unit suite sets NEO_CHROMA_DATABASE=neo-unit-test in the test-unit npm script (the reliable worker-reaching channel). Drop the now-unused CHROMA_TEST_DATABASE import.

Fail-closed: ChromaManager refuses the production database under UNIT_TEST_MODE, so a missed override fails loud -- never silent prod pollution. Verified: 21 ChromaManager specs pass (isolation + the fail-closed guard), incl. 'targets the isolated test database' + 'fails closed ... refuses production under UNIT_TEST_MODE'.

Drop the NEO_CHROMA_DATABASE row from the SSOT-lint BASELINE (lint: 3 baselined, green). The graph + dynamic-collection-name reshapes remain on #12451 (distinct risk).

Co-Authored-By: Neo Claude Opus <neo-claude-opus@neomjs.com>

* refactor(ai): config.template chroma leaf uses inline literal, not an imported constant (#12480)

Operator correction: config.template IS the SSOT — it must define its values inline, never import them. The prior reshape swapped the inline UNIT_TEST_MODE branch for an imported CHROMA_PRODUCTION_DATABASE constant — still 'config depending on an external var', the same antipattern. Use the literal 'default_database'; drop the chromaTestIsolation import entirely (config.template now imports zero config values).

chromaTestIsolation.mjs keeps its CHROMA_PRODUCTION_DATABASE constant as an INDEPENDENT defense-in-depth guard invariant — the ChromaManager fail-closed guard must not trust config (that is the point of the guard), so this is intentional, not a config-value duplication. 13/13 specs pass (config.template resolution + ChromaManager isolation + the fail-closed guard); lint green at 3 baselined.

Co-Authored-By: Neo Claude Opus <neo-claude-opus@neomjs.com>

* refactor(ai): chroma DB selection via 3 declarative config leaves + toggle (#12480)

Per @tobiu's design steer: config.template is the SSOT and the SSOT lint ENABLES env-driven values via the env-arg form — so the KISS shape is three declarative leaves: database ('default_database' literal), databaseTest ('neo-unit-test' literal), useTestDatabase (leaf(false,'UNIT_TEST_MODE','boolean') — env-driven toggle). config.template imports zero config values.

ChromaManager (consumer) reads the resolved toggle and selects databaseTest when on, else database — pure config reads, no inline process.env. Because BOTH names are config values (not an npm-script-only env var), 'npx playwright' without 'npm run test-unit' still toggles to the test DB and CANNOT bleed unit collections into production by construction — fixing the bleed risk in the prior env-var cut. The fail-closed guard now reads config (refuse if resolved === database while the toggle is on).

SSOT lint green at 3 baselined (the toggle leaf is declarative, not inline process.env). Tests updated + self-validating: the toggle resolves true under UNIT_TEST_MODE -> the resolver returns databaseTest, never default_database. 53 specs pass (ChromaManager + config.template + lint + chromaTestIsolation + purge + canonical-guard).

Co-Authored-By: Neo Claude Opus <neo-claude-opus@neomjs.com>

- 2026-06-04T11:10:15Z @neo-claude-opus cross-referenced by #12491
- 2026-06-04T11:49:53Z @neo-claude-opus cross-referenced by PR #12494
### @neo-claude-opus - 2026-06-04T11:53:49Z

**[reshape-note update — chroma #12481 merged, graph #12494 open]**

The `## Reshape` section's **env-relocation** approach (set test overrides like `NEO_MEMORY_DB_PATH=:memory:` in `playwright.config.unit.mjs`) is **superseded** by **config-resolution**, now established across the first two reshapes:

- **chroma** (#12480 → #12481, merged): `engines.chroma.useTestDatabase` toggle + `databaseTest` leaf; `ChromaManager` (single consumer) selects.
- **graph** (#12491 → #12494): a config-resolved `storagePaths.graph` **formula** (`useTestDatabase ? graphTest : graphProd`); the ~10 consumers read it unchanged.

Both keep the test value as a **config value** (not an env the runner must remember), resolved **by construction** — no "forgot the env → prod bleed" path. The remaining **dynamic collection-names** leaf (`NEO_MEMORY_COLLECTION_NAME`/`NEO_SESSION_COLLECTION_NAME`, still baselined) should follow this precedent + a per-worker bootstrap for the runtime-uniqueness — NOT the env-relocation.
